scenario: Buyer reserves an item from a moving sale

explainer.title: New to blueprints? Here's the whole idea.
explainer.body: This is the map of your product before you build any of it. We take one real moment, here a buyer reserving an item, break it into the steps they go through, and under each step we lay out everything that has to exist for it to work: what they touch, who's involved, the tech and rules behind the scenes, where it could break, and why you chose it that way. You get to see your entire idea on one page... before writing a line of code.
explainer.read: Read left → right for the journey. Read top → bottom to see everything standing behind each step.

steps.name: Discover the sale
steps.def: Taps a shared link to the sale page

steps.name: Browse the sale
steps.def: Scans the grid of items & prices

steps.name: Reserve
steps.def: Reservation made · place in line set

steps.name: Hold & queue
steps.def: System holds items, runs the timer

steps.name: Coordinate & pick up
steps.def: Messages seller, picks a time slot

process.6: Hold items · run timer · advance the line

policy.2: Show city only, never the full address
policy.6: Hold ends → next person is up
policy.7: Address only when seller says yes · hold → 72h

pitfall.1: Link preview won't render → looks sketchy
pitfall.2: A truck warning scares buyers off
pitfall.4: Code lands in spam → they give up
pitfall.5: They don't notice the timer running
pitfall.6: Hold ends silently → buyer feels ghosted

rationale.2: Privacy first, protect the seller's home
rationale.3: One basket per seller keeps pickups simple
rationale.4: A code, not a password = trust, no friction
rationale.5: A short line creates urgency without a dogpile
rationale.7: Scheduling turns a maybe into a commitment

question.1: Will the link preview always render right?
question.3: Will buyers grasp the one-seller rule?
question.5: Does the buyer realize the clock is ticking?
question.6: Message the buyer the moment a hold opens?

note.1: Most buyers are on phones, from Facebook
note.2: People look at photos first, read later
note.4: Often a different email than expected
